title: Validate archive index integrity and evidence hashes

sources.reason: Current archive metrics and index validation implementation.
Existing archive pairing and index regression coverage.
Documents archive integrity as a blocking check.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: valid index with matching archive pairs and hashes passes
missing index entry for an archived pair fails
duplicate sequence or work item identity fails
tampered hash or dangling path fails

riskAssessment.reason: A corrupted or incomplete archive index can hide immutable review evidence.

executionDecision.reason: The archive index validator has explicit inputs, failure modes, and local regression evidence.

preReviewWarnings: Hash and path checks must fail closed while preserving legacy archive readability.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Newly maintained archive index entries cover their Contract/Summary pairs exactly once.
Index paths, workItemId values, archiveSequence values, and stored SHA-256 hashes match the authoritative files.
Malformed, duplicated, dangling, or tampered index evidence fails the governance complexity check.
Legacy archives remain readable and immutable while newly archived evidence is validated strictly.
guidelines: Treat archived Contract and Summary files as authoritative evidence.
Fail closed on malformed index entries and hash mismatches.

intent.problem: The archive index currently checks only that referenced paths exist, so duplicate, incomplete, or tampered discovery records can pass while immutable archive evidence is inconsistent.
constraints: Do not rewrite historical archive records.
Keep archive counts observational; integrity remains blocking.
Preserve compatibility with legacy records that lack newer metadata where the repository already permits it.
intent.rationale: The index is a discovery manifest, but its claims must be verifiable against the authoritative archived files before reviewers rely on it.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
